meaning: n.圈套,陷阱,诡计,活板门,存水弯,汽水闸,(双轮)轻便马车vi.设圈套,设陷阱vt.诱捕,诱骗,计捉,设陷,坑害,使受限制
examples: A trap was laid, with fresh bait.
Suspecting nothing, he walked right into the trap.
Nathan's dog got caught in a trap.
"One always falls into the trap of going into the straight-replacement mode, " he says.
From the outside parking lot, restaurants and cafes, it looks like a tourist trap.
